name: Mel's Paella
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a paella pan, large skillet, or Dutch oven on medium-high heat.
2. Add the olive oil and cook the chicken and pork (cut into 1/4-inch slices) until browned and remove them from the pan.
3. Add onion, garlic, and chorizo; cook until the onion is translucent and chorizo is browned.
4. Stir in rice, cooking it a golden brown color.
5. Pour in wine and allow to come to a boil.
6. Reduce heat to a simmer.
7. In a separate saucepan or microwave, heat chicken broth, then add paprika and saffron.
8. Slowly incorporate the seasoned broth into the rice mixture; stir well.
9. Add cooked meats, onion, and garlic back in; stir again.
10. Arrange red bell pepper slices (1-inch thick) around the pan edge, forming a pattern.
11. Place remaining ingredients between and among the pepper slices.
12. Sprinkle peas over the top.
13. Loosely cover with aluminum foil and cook until all moisture is absorbed and seafood is cooked through.
14. Check clams and mussels; discard any that do not open after cooking. Ensure all clams and mussels are cleaned before use, discarding any already open ones.
